Fixed:

data.description: grava start → in_progress + work_sessions entry, grava stop → paused, ALREADY_IN_PROGRESS/NOT_IN_PROGRESS errors.

comments.text: Code Review (2026-04-02): 2 High, 4 Medium, 3 Low issues found. Fixed: 2 (H1+H2), Action items: 6.

[HIGH] startIssue never updated assignee column — ALREADY_IN_PROGRESS actor detection was unreliable. Fixed: SELECT FOR UPDATE inside WithAuditedTx, assignee=actor in UPDATE, stopped_at=NULL on restart. (start.go:34-88)

[HIGH] TOCTOU race condition: pre-read SELECT was outside transaction. Two concurrent agents could both read open and both proceed. Fixed: moved to SELECT...FOR UPDATE inside WithAuditedTx callback, matching claim.go pattern. (start.go, stop.go)
[MEDIUM] --json output path untested: grava start/stop --json has zero test coverage. Action item added. (start.go:119-123, stop.go:116-120)
[MEDIUM] DB_UNREACHABLE error path in pre-read untested: non-ErrNoRows DB errors not covered by unit tests. Action item added. (start.go:41-42, stop.go:41-42)
[MEDIUM] sprint-status.yaml missing from Dev Agent Record File List. Action item added.
Review APPROVED (2026-04-02). Story marked done. 2 HIGH issues fixed during review. 6 action items created for MEDIUM/LOW findings.

Session summary: implemented grava start/stop commands. Key changes: SELECT FOR UPDATE inside WithAuditedTx (TOCTOU fix), assignee set on start, stopped_at cleared on restart, db-start/db-stop rename, migration 005, SchemaVersion=5. All tests pass.

data.title: Task 1: Design and Create Work Session Tracking
[AI-Review][MEDIUM] Add DB_UNREACHABLE unit tests for startIssue/stopIssue pre-read
data.description: When tx.QueryRowContext returns a non-ErrNoRows error, the DB_UNREACHABLE branch is never hit by current tests. Add one test each to start_test.go and stop_test.go using mock.ExpectQuery(...).WillReturnError(errors.New("db down")). Files: pkg/cmd/issues/start_test.go, pkg/cmd/issues/stop_test.go (start.go:41-42, stop.go:41-42)

data.title: [AI-Review][MEDIUM] Add sprint-status.yaml to Dev Agent Record File List
data.description: The file _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml was modified (status in-progress -> review) but is absent from the story's Dev Agent Record File List, breaking change traceability.

data.title: [AI-Review][LOW] Guard grava start against terminal-status transitions
data.description: startIssue only checks currentStatus == in_progress. Issues with status=done/archived/closed can be transitioned to in_progress. Add a guard after the in_progress check returning INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION if status is not open. File: pkg/cmd/issues/start.go:47-52

data.title: [AI-Review][LOW] Add idx_stopped_at index to migration 005
data.description: Migration 005 adds idx_started_at but not idx_stopped_at. Cycle time range queries (WHERE stopped_at BETWEEN ? AND ?) will full-scan as the table grows. File: pkg/migrate/migrations/005_add_work_session_columns.sql

data.title: [AI-Review][LOW] Document Epic 2 paused vs open divergence in Dev Notes
data.description: Epic 02 (line 118) and grava-1073 description both say grava stop -> paused. Story AC#2, architecture audit table, and implementation all use open. The paused status does not exist in current schema validation. Add a Dev Notes entry clarifying the architecture is authoritative and paused was an early draft term.

data.title: [AI-Review][MEDIUM] Add --json output tests for grava start/stop
data.description: grava start/stop --json output paths are untested. Add TestStartCmd_JSON and TestStopCmd_JSON to commands_test.go verifying the NFR5 JSON shapes: {id,status,started_at} and {id,status,stopped_at}. Files: pkg/cmd/commands_test.go (start.go:119-123, stop.go:116-120)

data.title: 7.4: Automatic Sync on git pull via Post-Merge Hook

data.title: fix: initConfig prints to stderr in --json mode (story 1.1)
data.description: pkg/cmd/root.go:189 — initConfig uses fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, 'Using config file: ...') on every invocation including --json mode. Callers using 2>&1 get mixed non-JSON + JSON output, breaking pipeline consumers. Fix: guard with if !outputJSON.

chore: remove dead code guard if gravaDir != empty (story 1.1)
data.description: pkg/cmd/root.go:82 — the 'if gravaDir != empty' guard around CheckSchemaVersion is dead code. gravaDir is guaranteed non-empty by this line because ResolveGravaDir() returns an error (exits early) if it would be empty. Leftover from the previous buggy implementation where the guard was needed. Remove it.

data.description: grava import: JSONL hash vs Dolt state check, single TX (all-or-nothing), IMPORT_CONFLICT on uncommitted local changes, rollback on crash with safe-to-retry message.

data.title: fix: SchemaVersion=3 but 4 migrations exist (story 1.1)
data.description: pkg/utils/schema.go:15 — const SchemaVersion = 3 but pkg/migrate/migrations/ has 4 files (001–004). Task 4 spec says 'match migration count'. After grava init runs all 4 migrations, WriteSchemaVersion writes 3 — schema state is misrepresented by 1. Fix: set SchemaVersion = 4.

comments.text:
Round 2 code review complete. 7 new findings — action items created, story moved back to in-progress.

Issues created:
- grava-2e00 [CRITICAL] SchemaVersion=3 but 4 migrations exist
- grava-16c3 [HIGH] initConfig prints to stderr in --json mode
- grava-a087 [HIGH] fmt.Printf in show/dep tree helpers bypass cobra output

- grava-f9a1 [MEDIUM] tx_test.go defer db.Close() missing nolint:errcheck

Previous round: 14 findings all resolved. These 7 are fresh.

Round 2 review findings all fixed. Commit: 0eecb467eec2b36d2bacfd57efaafa01501ac231

Fixed (7/7):
[CRITICAL] SchemaVersion 3→4 to match 4 migration files
[HIGH] initConfig stderr now guarded by !outputJSON
[HIGH] renderTreeNode(show.go) + printTree/printImpactTree(dep.go) pass io.Writer
[MEDIUM] Dead-code guard 'if gravaDir != empty' removed from root.go
[MEDIUM] tx_test.go + claim_test.go defer db.Close() nolint:errcheck added

Story back to review status.

Session Summary (Round-3 Review Fixes): Addressed 5 adversarial findings.
- dep.go: depPathCmd.RunE fmt.Printf → cmd.Printf (missed in round-2)
- util.go: 6x fmt.Errorf → gravaerrors.New for structured --json error codes
- schema_test.go: hardcoded 3 → utils.SchemaVersion; added migration file count sentinel test
- devlog.go: replaced full implementation with true no-op stubs (no imports, no I/O)

Round-4 verification review: PASS. Story 1.1 is done.

data.title: fix: fmt.Printf in show/dep tree helpers bypass cobra output (story 1.1)
data.description: pkg/cmd/show.go:193 renderTreeNode and pkg/cmd/dep.go:178+ renderBlockedNode/renderImpactNode use fmt.Printf to process stdout, bypassing cmd.OutOrStdout(). Previous review fixed fmt.Println at show.go:141 but missed fmt.Printf at 193. In --json mode, grava show --tree and dep tree commands emit mixed text+JSON to stdout. Fix: pass io.Writer to both helpers.

data.description: pkg/utils/schema.go:27 — uses deprecated os.IsNotExist(err) API (pre-Go 1.13). Modern idiom is errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist). Same issue was flagged in Story 1.3 review for gitexclude.go and should be fixed consistently across both files.

data.description: pkg/dolt/tx_test.go:66,93,118 — three defer db.Close() calls are missing the //nolint:errcheck comment. golangci-lint errcheck fires on all three lines. Rest of codebase consistently uses defer db.Close() //nolint:errcheck pattern.

data.title: 4.3: Query Blockers for a Specific Issue
data.description: grava blocked <id>: upstream blocker list {id,title,status,assignee}, JSON array, ISSUE_NOT_FOUND error.

data.title: Code review findings: story 1.3 (medium/low severity)
data.description:
Remaining medium/low findings from adversarial review of story 1.3 (Worktree Resolver, Named Functions & Test Harness). Critical/high findings already fixed in the same session.

Finding 6 (MEDIUM): testutil.MockStore duplicates pkg/dolt.MockStore without deprecation notice. Two competing mock implementations exist in the same module. Recommend deprecating pkg/dolt.MockStore or adding a doc comment pointing users to testutil.MockStore.

Finding 7 (MEDIUM): claimIssue hardcodes OldValue: {status: open} in the AuditEvent slice before the transaction SELECT executes. Safe today because fn returns error for non-open statuses, but a future change to WithAuditedTx semantics (e.g., always log on rollback) would produce incorrect audit records. Add a comment explaining the ordering assumption.

Finding 8 (LOW): pkg/utils/gitexclude.go line 18 uses deprecated os.IsNotExist instead of errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist). Minor style inconsistency with the rest of the codebase.

data.title: 11.2: TS-01 — Basic Issue Lifecycle (Epic 2 Gate)
data.description: Scenario: create → update → label → comment → drop; structured JSON at each step; <30s; exit 0.

data.title: 4.1: Establish and Remove Dependency Relationships
data.description: grava dep --add/--remove, WithDeadlockRetry, CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY structured error at write time.
